name: Honey Lemon Sponge Cake (Kastutera)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F. Line the bottom of a 10-inch tube pan (with a removable bottom if possible) with parchment paper and grease it. In a large bowl, whisk together 1 cup sugar, honey, vanilla extract, lemon extract, and salt until combined. Add egg yolks one at a time and whisk until smooth. Place the bowl over a pan of simmering water and beat with an electric mixer on medium-high speed for about 5 minutes, or until the mixture is pale yellow and has doubled in volume. Sift in the flour and gently fold into the batter.
2. In another large bowl, beat egg whites with an electric mixer on low speed until foamy. Gradually add 1 tablespoon sugar and cream of tartar while continuing to beat until stiff peaks form but are not dry. Gently fold the beaten egg whites into the batter in thirds using a spatula. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and tap it gently on the counter to release any air bubbles.
3. Bake in the middle rack of the oven for 35 to 45 minutes, or until the cake is golden brown and the sides pull away slightly from the pan while the top remains flat and feels spongy when pressed with a finger. Allow the cake to cool for 20 minutes before running a knife around the edge to loosen it. Carefully remove the cake from the pan and parchment paper, then allow it to cool completely before serving or storing airtight.
